input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.2.7.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.2.7.3
            TITLE: installation of all anti-submarine belts must go in a straight line from the anti-submarine belt

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.7.3: Installation of all Anti-Submarine Belts must go in a straight line from the Anti-Submarine Belt
T.2.7.3 Installation of all Anti-Submarine Belts must go in a straight line from the Anti-Submarine Belt 
Mounting Point(s) without touching any hole in the seat or any other intermediate structure 
until they reach: 
a. The release buckle for the 5 Point Harness mounting per T.2.7.1 
b. The first point where the belt touches the driver’s body for the 6 Point Harness mounting 
per T.2.7.2

final_answer: rule T.2.7.3: Installation of all Anti-Submarine Belts must go in a straight line from the Anti-Submarine Belt Mounting Point(s) without touching any hole in the seat or any other intermediate structure until they reach: a. The release buckle for the 5 Point Harness mounting per T.2.7.1 b. The first point where the belt touches the driver’s body for the 6 Point Harness mounting per T.2.7.2
